creator: freebsd@dev.thsi.be
text:
Hello, I've proposed more narrower approach in D43861 which I strongly recommend to be approved so that the much needed OCaml upgrade doesn't get delayed ad-vitam.

It is narrower because it is only fixing the build issue on the 4.14. OCaml/Camlp5 versions. The pa_macro bundled copy is replaced by the one provided from camlp5 and the following syntax errors are simple missing semi-colons (probably caused by a minor subtle changed that occured in camlp4/camlp5 during the last 20 years, after the bundle got taken...).

I suppose that the port maintainer can decide however/whenever he wants to keep his package updated w.r.t. newer geneweb releases. That's not really my business, but, in my opinion, whatever gets done here shouldn't prevent to have the core ocaml packages getting upgraded -- which is what I have done in D43861.

Side node: the real solution for upstream would be to get rid of that useless preprocressing crap. The whole thing looks to have evolved from a language theory class from 30 years ago...  And upstream doesn't seem very much into the "release" thing...  very much into the genealogy, that's a given ;-)
